{{commons category|Category:Tsuno-jinja}} [[EN:Tsuno Shrine]] [[FR:Tsuno-jinja]] [[JA:都農神社]] [[SIMPLE:Tsuno Shrine]] [[ZH:都農神社]] {{wikidata link|Q3541675}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Tsuno Shrine |Image = [[File:Tsuno-jinja keidai.JPG|270px]]
Precincts (prayer hall on the left, Kumano Shrine, a subsidiary shrine on the right) |Location = {{jalink|Miyazaki Prefecture|Q130300}}{{jalink|Koyu District|Q1132169}}{{jalink|Tsuno Town|Q1205974}}Oaza Kawakita 13294 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 degrees minutes, seconds|32|15|47.2|N|131|33|31.2|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Okuninushi|Q276944|Okumi no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Shaku = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{jalink|Hyuga Province|Q513737}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}
Old {{jalink|Modern company rank system #government shrine|Q712534|Kokuheishosha}}
{{jalink|Beppo Shrine|Q10898274}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}}6 years before enthronement |Main shrine = Ikkensha {{jalink|Raguzou|Q210684}}Copper plate roof |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{jalink|December 5th|Q2300}} |Shinto ritual = Summer festival ({{jalink|August 1st|Q2788}}-{{jalink|August 2nd|Q2785|2nd}})
Winter festival ({{jalink|December 4th|Q2310}}-{{jalink|December 5th|Q2300|5th}})|Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyazaki|label=Tsuno Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Tsuno-jinja minamitorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] ``Tsuno Shrine'' is located in {{jalink|Miyazaki Prefecture|Q130300}}{{jalink|Koyu District|Q1132169}}{{jalink|Tsuno Town|Q1205974}}. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, {{jalink|Hyuga Country|Q513737}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}. {{jalink|Modern Shrine Ranking System|Q712534|Old Shrine Ranking}} is {{jalink|Modern Shrine Ranking System|Q712534|Old Shrine}} is {{jalink|Modern Shrine Ranking System#Government Shrine|Q712534|Kokuhei-kosha}}, and currently it is {{jalink|Beppedo Shrine|Q10898274}} of {{jalink|Shinto Honcho|Q908077}}. Before {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, it was also called ``Miyazaki-sha'' and ``Miyazaki-miya''. It is also called ``Ichinomiya Shrine'' locally. === Enshrined God === * '''{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōmi Takamikoto}}''' (Onamuchi Mikoto) *: Same as Okuninushi no Mikoto. Since it has been the guardian deity of the Tsuno-cho area since ancient times, it is said that it was dedicated to the deity of the land. Furthermore, ``{{jalink|Kinbukuro|Q17213633}}'' has the text ``Tsu no Daimyojin.''There is a theory that this article in ``Kinbukuro'' is an anecdote from ``{{jalink|Fudōki|Q1194534|Hyuga Province Fudoki}},'' but this is being questioned (Iwanami Japanese Classical Literature Compendium ``Fudōki''). , ``{{jalink|Jinzoe壒壒鈔|Q30928164}}'' adds an additional note to it and states that it is a miraculous cure for {{jalink|smallpox|Q12214|smallpox}}. It was also believed that it would remove insect damage from the fields. === History === === Founding === According to the shrine's legend, {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}} began worshiping the shrine six years before his ascension to the throne, when the Emperor was en route to the {{jalink|Jinmu Tosei|Q11590444|Eastern Conquest}}. . In addition, an article in ``Jinzoe Ibukuro'' that says that {{jalink|Empress Jingu|Q232803}} solicited ``Tuno Daimyojin'' as a guardian deity for boats during the {{jalink|Sanhan Conquest|Q11249435}} This article is already in ``Jinbukuro'' and, as mentioned in the previous note, is regarded as an anecdote from ``Fudoki''. Citing , it is reported that the shrine was first built by the Empress after her triumphant return. Similarly, in ``{{jalink|Jinbukuro|Q17213633}}'', when Empress Jingu returned from the Sanhan Conquest, when she shot a bow at ``Bama-no-mine'', a black head appeared out of the soil, so she dug it up in the mountain, and two people, a man and a woman, appeared, and she summoned them as {{jalink|jinjin|Q11588676}}. In addition to ruins dating back to the {{jalink|Jomon period|Q52824}}, there are more than 20 ancient tombs (Tsuno kofun group) such as {{jalink|circular tumulus|Q11394747}} and {{jalink|keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718}}, and pottery and stone tools have been excavated near our company's shrine. It is known that people lived there from an early time, and the history of our company as a guardian deity dates back to ancient times. === Outline === {{jalink|Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jowa}} In the 4th year ({{jalink|837|Q25093}}), he was promoted to a government shrine, and in the 10th year of Jowa ({{jalink|843|Q23469}}), he was given the rank of Junior Fifth Rank (hereinafter referred to as ``{{jalink|Shoku Nippon Ki|Q1041301}}''), {{jalink|Tenan (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} In 2nd year ({{jalink|858|Q30371}}), he was promoted to Jushii (Junior Fourth Rank) ("{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}"). In "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}, ``'''Tsuno Shrine'''' is written in {{jalink|Hyuga Province|Q513737}}{{jalink|Koyu District|Q1132169}}, and it is listed as {{jalink|Shinouchi Shrine}}. It is said that at that time it was the largest shrine in Hyuga Province, with a magnificent shrine building and vast grounds. It is said that this position caused a political and religious conflict with {{jalink|Tsuman Shrine|Q3541658}}, which is listed in Tsunō Shrine in ``Japanese Gods''. As will be explained later, the details are not clear because the ancient documents were destroyed.It is certain that our company was revered not only by the common people but also by the local feudal lords as ``Ichinomiya of Hyuga Province'', and in {{jalink|Tenbun (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}}18 ({{jalink|1549|Q6554}}), the shrine building was rebuilt by {{jalink|Yoshisuke Ito|Q6095349}}. Later, in {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}}6 ({{jalink|1578|Q6707}}), during the conflict between the Otomo and Shimazu armies, {{jalink|Otomoyoshi It gradually declined after the shrine building, shrine treasures, and ancient documents were all destroyed in the fires of Chin|Q1185032|Otomo Sorin}} and {{jalink|Yoshihisa Shimazu|Q955727}}. In {{jalink|Enpo|Q1145735}} 3rd year ({{jalink|1675|Q7624}}), ``After that, there was no one to collect it, and it became a small shrine, unknown to the name, and only worshiped as a deity.'' The situation was as follows.After that, it was rebuilt in {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}} 5th year ({{jalink|1692|Q7685}}) by {{jalink|Takanabe clan|Q3034541|Takanabe clan lord}} {{jalink|Akizuki Tanemasa|Q11595121}} Since 20 koku of shrine territory was donated in the 14th year of Genroku ({{jalink|1701|Q6814}}), it has been revered by successive {{jalink|Takanabe clan|Q3034541|Takanabe feudal lord}} throughout the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}. Torii dedicated by {{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}} in 12 years ({{jalink|1727|Q6942}}) by {{jalink|Tanehiro Akizuki|Q11595118}}. The construction of the shrine in {{jalink|1736|Q6989}} by {{jalink|Tanemi Akizuki|Q11595125}} and {{ jalink|Enkyo|Q1183728}} 2nd year ({{jalink|1745|Q7586}}) Torii gate construction, {{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}}6th year ({{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}}) k|1859|Q7687}})'s {{jalink|Akizuki Tanekin|Q11595126|Dosokuin}}, the shrine building was constructed, and the lords of the domain often visited the shrine, made deputations, and offered offerings.{{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}4 ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}) In {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534}}{{jalink|Modern company rank system#government company|Q7 12534|Kokuhei-kosha}}, and in {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}9 ({{jalink|1934|Q18714}}), the area of the shrine was expanded and improved as part of the ``2600th Anniversary of Emperor Jimmu's Transfer to the East.'' After the war, it belonged to {{jalink|Shrine Honcho|Q908077}}, and in 1948 ({{jalink|1948|Q5165}}) It became Omote Shrine|Q10898274}}, and the main shrine was rebuilt in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}19 ({{jalink|2007|Q2024}}). === God level === * In August of {{jalink|Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jowa}} 4th year ({{jalink|837|Q25093}}), it was promoted to a government shrine (``{{jalink|Shoku Nihon Koki|Q1041301}}'') - It is written as ``Shitonojin.'' *September 19, 10th year of Jowa ({{jalink|843|Q23469}}), from no rank to junior fifth rank ("Shoku Nihon Koki") - Written as "Tsuno Kojin". * {{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} October 22, 2nd year ({{jalink|858|Q30371}}), from the upper fifth rank to the upper fourth rank (``{{jalink|Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981}}'') === Precincts === [[File:Tsuno-jinja honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|main hall]] The main shrine is {{jalink|Nagaruzou|Q210684|Ikkensha Nagaruzou}} with a copper plate roof and {{jalink|Chigi/Katsuoki|Q87220234}}. It was rebuilt in 2007 and largely follows the shape of the old main shrine. The old main shrine was rebuilt in 1986 with a donation from Kinosuke Kono, and it has now been relocated and serves as the main shrine of Kumano Shrine, a subsidiary shrine. The worship hall, like the main shrine, was rebuilt in 2007. It is a {{jalink|irimoya-zukuri |Q855025}} with 5 rooms in the front and 3 spaces on the sides, and a {{jalink|gable-zukuri |Q13308575}} tsumairi shrine with a depth of 2 ken in the center of the back. The roof is made of copper sheets, with a {{jalink|gable #form of gables|Q4881511|staggered gable}} on the front, and below that is a three-room {{jalink|facing gable|Q11415970}} with a gable and a gable in the center. The old worship hall has been relocated and is now used as a kagura hall. In ancient times, the Ichi-no-Torii gate was located near Tsuno High School ({{Coord|32|15|06.90|N|131|33|43.93|E|region:JP-45_type:landmark|name=Ichi-no-Torii ruins}}), and its size shows the power of the past. File:Tsuno-jinja haiden.JPG|Shrine of worship File:Tsuno-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kaguraden
Old worship hall.File:Tsuno-jinja shinmon.JPG|Shinmon File:Tsuno-jinja kitaootorii.JPG|North Exit Otorii === Setsusha === === Okumiya === [[File:Tsuno-jinja Taki-jinja.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Taki Shrine
Fudo Falls in the background. ]] * '''Taki Shrine'' ** Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Nakamikami|Q644201|Takani no Mikoto}} *: It is located approximately 1km northeast of our company ({{Coord|32|16|21.07|N|131|33|04.20|E|region:JP-45_type:landmark|name=Okumiya: Taki Shrine}}). It enshrines the ``Fudo-no-taki'' waterfall that flows behind the shrine. === Sessha === *Sujin Shrine ** Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoomikoto}} *: Enshrined on the left side of the main shrine. * Asumamiku Shrine, Temasyuu Shrine ** Deity enshrined: {{jalink|Ashinazuchi Tenazuchi|Q643763|Ashima Nyuu no Mikoto}} (Ashima Nyuu Shrine), {{jalink|Ashinazuchi Tenazuchi|Q643763|Temami Nyu no Mikoto}} (Temami Nyu no Mikoto) *: To the right of the main shrine, two shrines are enshrined in one place. File:Tsuno-jinja Susanoo-jinja.JPG|Sujin Shrine File:Tsuno-jinja Ashinazuchi & Tenazuchi-jinja.JPG|Ashimyu Shrine/Temazuchi Shrine === Suesha === * Inari Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Uka no Mitama no Mikoto}} * Atago Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Fireborn Spirit}} * Kumano Shrine - Deities enshrined: {{jalink|Kumano Hayatama no Mikoto||Hatama no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kikurihime no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kikurihime no Mikoto|Q11619891|Kikurihime no Mikoto}}. The main building is the former main building of the main office. File:Tsuno-jinja Atago-jinja.JPG|Atago Shrine File:Tsuno-jinja Kumano-jinja.JPG|Kumano Shrine
The shrine building is the former main building of the headquarters. === Festival === === Annual festival ===
List of annual festivals
* Monthly ** Monthly festival (1 day) - Small festival Festivals are according to [http://www1.bbiq.jp/tunojinjya/ Annual festival list] (official website). * January ** Saitan Festival (January 1st) - Middle Festival ** New Year's Festival (January 2nd) - Small Festival ** Genshi Festival (January 3rd) - Middle Festival *February ** Setsubun Oishi Ceremony (Sunday before February 3rd) - Small festival ** Setsubun Festival (February 3rd) - Small festival ** Kigen Festival (February 11th) - Middle Festival ** '''Prayer Festival'' (February 17th) - Grand Festival ** Kumano Shrine Annual Festival (February 21st) - Small Festival * March ** Peach Blossom Festival (March 3rd) - Small Festival ** Atago Shrine Annual Festival (March 5th) - Small Festival ** Inari Shrine Spring Festival (March 17th) - Small Festival ** Taki Shrine Spring Festival (March 20th) - Small Festival ** Tsuno Soreisha Spring Sorei Festival (March 20th) - Middle Festival *April ** Kangakusai (April 10th) - Small festival ** Showa Festival (April 29th) - Small Festival ** Flower offering festival (April 29th) - Small festival * May** Kamioi Festival (May 5th) - Small Festival ** Iris Festival (Boy's Festival) (May 5th) - Small festival ** Kokuheisha Ranking Anniversary Festival (May 14th) - Small Festival * June ** Summer Purification Ceremony (June 30th) * July ** Transference Memorial Festival/Tanabata Festival (July 7th) - Small Festival ** Summer Festival Eve (July 31st) - Small Festival *August ** '''Summer Festival Mikoshi Festival'' (August 1st) - Grand Festival ** '''Summer Festival Return Festival'' (August 2nd) - Grand Festival *September ** Chongyang Festival (Longevity Festival) (September 9th) - Small Festival ** Taki Shrine Autumn Festival (September 23rd) - Small Festival ** Tsuno Soreisha Autumn Sorei Festival (September 23rd) - Middle Festival * October ** Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (October 7th) - Small Festival ** Kannaho Festival New Grain Thanksgiving (October 17th) - Middle Festival *November ** Meiji Festival (November 3rd) - Middle Festival ** Kamioi Festival (November 7th) - Small Festival ** '''Ninamesai''' (November 23rd) - Grand Festival *December ** Winter Festival Eve (December 4th) - Small Festival ** '''Reitaisai'' (December 5th) - Taisai ** Tencho Festival (December 23rd) - Middle Festival ** Soot Festival (December 27th) - Small Festival ** Grand Purification Ceremony/New Year's Eve Festival (December 31st) - Small Festival
=== Main festivals === * Summer Festival ({{jalink|August 1st|Q2788}}-{{jalink|August 2nd|Q2785|2nd}}) *: Also called the ``Goshinko Festival,'' {{jalink|Mikoshi|Q979940}} is paraded around Tsuno Town. It is said that the name comes from the fact that Empress Jingu brought the enshrined deity of the company to her boat as a guardian deity during the Sanhan Conquest, and there was a time when it was discontinued, but it was revived in {{jalink|Tenpo|Q1369045}}3 ({{jalink|1832|Q7584}}). * Winter Festival ({{jalink|December 4th|Q2310}}-{{jalink|December 5th|Q2300|5th}}) *: Kagura is played throughout the night, and the annual festival is held on the 5th. Also, on that day, it is said that if a woman suffering from breast ailments offers a ladle, she will be cured. === Cultural assets === === Tsuno-cho designated natural monument === [[File:Tsuno-jinja kusu.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Kusu (Town-designated Natural Monument)
Standing in front of the amulet awarding place. ]] * {{jalink|camphor tree|Q158722|camphor tree}} at Tsuno Shrine === Others === * Mask (wooden) - {{jalink|Qin Hesheng|Q5680877}} dedication. * Armor (Konsui) - {{jalink|Taneki Akizuki|Q11595123}} Dedicated. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Miyazaki Prefecture|Q130300}}{{jalink|Koyu District|Q1132169}}{{jalink|Tsuno Town|Q1205974}}Oaza Kawakita 13294 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{jalink|Kyushu Railway|Q498366|JR Kyushu}}{{jalink|Nippo Main Line|Q1052288}} {{jalink|Tsuno Station|Q5357711}} **Walk: about 20 minutes ** Car: Approximately 5 minutes '''Surroundings''' * Tsuno Tumulus Group === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history book * “Japanese Historical Place Name System: Place Names of Miyazaki Prefecture” ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}) Tsuno Shrine section, Tsuno Town, Koyu District * Meizo Nakamura "Tsuno Shrine" ({{jalink|Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596}} ed. "Japanese Gods -Shrines and Sacred Sites- 1 Kyushu" {{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}) * Supervised by {{jalink|Naoichi Miyaji|Q11453535}} and {{jalink|Yuyoshi Saeki|Q11382847}} "Great Dictionary of Shinto, reduced edition" {{jalink|Rinkawa Shoten|Q11612454}}, 1969 * Edited by Shikinaisha Research Association, Shikinaisha Research Report, Volume 24, Saikaido {{jalink|Kogakukan University}} Publishing Department, 1978 === Related books === * Motohiko Anzu and Yoshihiko Umeda editors and supervisors, "Shinto Dictionary", Jinja Shinposha, 1968, p. 39 * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masanori Toki, "Dictionary of Shrines" {{jalink|Tokyodo Publishing|Q11524645}}, 1979, pp. 233-234 === External link === {{Commonscat|Tsuno-jinja}} * [https://tunojinjya6.webnode.jp Hyuga Province Ichinomiya Tsuno Shrine] (official website) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/640102.html Tsuno Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsunoshinshiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsuno Shrine (Q3541675)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsuno Shrine (Q3541675)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hyūga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮崎県の神社]] [[Category:都農町の建築物]] [[Category:都農町の歴史]] [[Category:日向国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:国幣小社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:日向国|社1]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsuno Shrine (Q3541675)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsuno Shrine (Q3541675)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tsuno Shrine (Q3541675)}}[[Category:qq]]

Tsuno Shrine |Image = [[File:Tsuno-jinja keidai.JPG|270px]]
Precincts (prayer hall on the left, Kumano Shrine, a subsidiary shrine on the right) |Location = {{jalink|Miyazaki Prefecture|Q130300}}{{jalink|Koyu District|Q1132169}}{{jalink|Tsuno Town|Q1205974}}Oaza Kawakita 13294 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 degrees minutes, seconds|32|15|47.2|N|131|33|31.2|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Okuninushi|Q276944|Okumi no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Shaku = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)

{{jalink|Beppo Shrine|Q10898274}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}}6 years before enthronement |Main shrine = Ikkensha {{jalink|Raguzou|Q210684}}Copper plate roof |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{jalink|December 5th|Q2300}} |Shinto ritual = Summer festival ({{jalink|August 1st|Q2788}}-{{jalink|August 2nd|Q2785|2nd}})
Winter festival ({{jalink|December 4th|Q2310}}-{{jalink|December 5th|Q2300|5th}})|Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyazaki|label=Tsuno Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Tsuno-jinja minamitorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] ``Tsuno Shrine'' is located in {{jalink|Miyazaki Prefecture|Q130300}}{{jalink|Koyu District|Q1132169}}{{jalink|Tsuno Town|Q1205974}}. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, {{jalink|Hyuga Country|Q513737}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}. {{jalink|Modern Shrine Ranking System|Q712534|Old Shrine Ranking}} is {{jalink|Modern Shrine Ranking System|Q712534|Old Shrine}} is {{jalink|Modern Shrine Ranking System#Government Shrine|Q712534|Kokuhei-kosha}}, and currently it is {{jalink|Beppedo Shrine|Q10898274}} of {{jalink|Shinto Honcho|Q908077}}. Before {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, it was also called ``Miyazaki-sha'' and ``Miyazaki-miya''. It is also called ``Ichinomiya Shrine'' locally. === Enshrined God === * '''{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōmi Takamikoto}}''' (Onamuchi Mikoto) *: Same as Okuninushi no Mikoto. Since it has been the guardian deity of the Tsuno-cho area since ancient times, it is said that it was dedicated to the deity of the land. Furthermore, ``{{jalink|Kinbukuro|Q17213633}}'' has the text ``Tsu no Daimyojin.''There is a theory that this article in ``Kinbukuro'' is an anecdote from ``{{jalink|Fudōki|Q1194534|Hyuga Province Fudoki}},'' but this is being questioned (Iwanami Japanese Classical Literature Compendium ``Fudōki''). , ``{{jalink|Jinzoe壒壒鈔|Q30928164}}'' adds an additional note to it and states that it is a miraculous cure for {{jalink|smallpox|Q12214|smallpox}}. It was also believed that it would remove insect damage from the fields. === History === === Founding === According to the shrine's legend, {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}} began worshiping the shrine six years before his ascension to the throne, when the Emperor was en route to the {{jalink|Jinmu Tosei|Q11590444|Eastern Conquest}}. . In addition, an article in ``Jinzoe Ibukuro'' that says that {{jalink|Empress Jingu|Q232803}} solicited ``Tuno Daimyojin'' as a guardian deity for boats during the {{jalink|Sanhan Conquest|Q11249435}} This article is already in ``Jinbukuro'' and, as mentioned in the previous note, is regarded as an anecdote from ``Fudoki''. Citing , it is reported that the shrine was first built by the Empress after her triumphant return. Similarly, in ``{{jalink|Jinbukuro|Q17213633}}'', when Empress Jingu returned from the Sanhan Conquest, when she shot a bow at ``Bama-no-mine'', a black head appeared out of the soil, so she dug it up in the mountain, and two people, a man and a woman, appeared, and she summoned them as {{jalink|jinjin|Q11588676}}. In addition to ruins dating back to the {{jalink|Jomon period|Q52824}}, there are more than 20 ancient tombs (Tsuno kofun group) such as {{jalink|circular tumulus|Q11394747}} and {{jalink|keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718}}, and pottery and stone tools have been excavated near our company's shrine. It is known that people lived there from an early time, and the history of our company as a guardian deity dates back to ancient times. === Outline === {{jalink|Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jowa}} In the 4th year ({{jalink|837|Q25093}}), he was promoted to a government shrine, and in the 10th year of Jowa ({{jalink|843|Q23469}}), he was given the rank of Junior Fifth Rank (hereinafter referred to as ``{{jalink|Shoku Nippon Ki|Q1041301}}''), {{jalink|Tenan (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} In 2nd year ({{jalink|858|Q30371}}), he was promoted to Jushii (Junior Fourth Rank) ("{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}"). In "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}, ``'''Tsuno Shrine'''' is written in {{jalink|Hyuga Province|Q513737}}{{jalink|Koyu District|Q1132169}}, and it is listed as {{jalink|Shinouchi Shrine}}. It is said that at that time it was the largest shrine in Hyuga Province, with a magnificent shrine building and vast grounds. It is said that this position caused a political and religious conflict with {{jalink|Tsuman Shrine|Q3541658}}, which is listed in Tsunō Shrine in ``Japanese Gods''. As will be explained later, the details are not clear because the ancient documents were destroyed.It is certain that our company was revered not only by the common people but also by the local feudal lords as ``Ichinomiya of Hyuga Province'', and in {{jalink|Tenbun (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}}18 ({{jalink|1549|Q6554}}), the shrine building was rebuilt by {{jalink|Yoshisuke Ito|Q6095349}}. Later, in {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}}6 ({{jalink|1578|Q6707}}), during the conflict between the Otomo and Shimazu armies, {{jalink|Otomoyoshi It gradually declined after the shrine building, shrine treasures, and ancient documents were all destroyed in the fires of Chin|Q1185032|Otomo Sorin}} and {{jalink|Yoshihisa Shimazu|Q955727}}. In {{jalink|Enpo|Q1145735}} 3rd year ({{jalink|1675|Q7624}}), ``After that, there was no one to collect it, and it became a small shrine, unknown to the name, and only worshiped as a deity.'' The situation was as follows.After that, it was rebuilt in {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}} 5th year ({{jalink|1692|Q7685}}) by {{jalink|Takanabe clan|Q3034541|Takanabe clan lord}} {{jalink|Akizuki Tanemasa|Q11595121}} Since 20 koku of shrine territory was donated in the 14th year of Genroku ({{jalink|1701|Q6814}}), it has been revered by successive {{jalink|Takanabe clan|Q3034541|Takanabe feudal lord}} throughout the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}. Torii dedicated by {{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}} in 12 years ({{jalink|1727|Q6942}}) by {{jalink|Tanehiro Akizuki|Q11595118}}. The construction of the shrine in {{jalink|1736|Q6989}} by {{jalink|Tanemi Akizuki|Q11595125}} and {{ jalink|Enkyo|Q1183728}} 2nd year ({{jalink|1745|Q7586}}) Torii gate construction, {{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}}6th year ({{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}}) k|1859|Q7687}})'s {{jalink|Akizuki Tanekin|Q11595126|Dosokuin}}, the shrine building was constructed, and the lords of the domain often visited the shrine, made deputations, and offered offerings.{{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}4 ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}) In {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534}}{{jalink|Modern company rank system#government company|Q7 12534|Kokuhei-kosha}}, and in {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}9 ({{jalink|1934|Q18714}}), the area of the shrine was expanded and improved as part of the ``2600th Anniversary of Emperor Jimmu's Transfer to the East.'' After the war, it belonged to {{jalink|Shrine Honcho|Q908077}}, and in 1948 ({{jalink|1948|Q5165}}) It became Omote Shrine|Q10898274}}, and the main shrine was rebuilt in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}19 ({{jalink|2007|Q2024}}). === God level === * In August of {{jalink|Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jowa}} 4th year ({{jalink|837|Q25093}}), it was promoted to a government shrine (``{{jalink|Shoku Nihon Koki|Q1041301}}'') - It is written as ``Shitonojin.'' *September 19, 10th year of Jowa ({{jalink|843|Q23469}}), from no rank to junior fifth rank ("Shoku Nihon Koki") - Written as "Tsuno Kojin". * {{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} October 22, 2nd year ({{jalink|858|Q30371}}), from the upper fifth rank to the upper fourth rank (``{{jalink|Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981}}'') === Precincts === [[File:Tsuno-jinja honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|main hall]] The main shrine is {{jalink|Nagaruzou|Q210684|Ikkensha Nagaruzou}} with a copper plate roof and {{jalink|Chigi/Katsuoki|Q87220234}}. It was rebuilt in 2007 and largely follows the shape of the old main shrine. The old main shrine was rebuilt in 1986 with a donation from Kinosuke Kono, and it has now been relocated and serves as the main shrine of Kumano Shrine, a subsidiary shrine. The worship hall, like the main shrine, was rebuilt in 2007. It is a {{jalink|irimoya-zukuri |Q855025}} with 5 rooms in the front and 3 spaces on the sides, and a {{jalink|gable-zukuri |Q13308575}} tsumairi shrine with a depth of 2 ken in the center of the back. The roof is made of copper sheets, with a {{jalink|gable #form of gables|Q4881511|staggered gable}} on the front, and below that is a three-room {{jalink|facing gable|Q11415970}} with a gable and a gable in the center. The old worship hall has been relocated and is now used as a kagura hall. In ancient times, the Ichi-no-Torii gate was located near Tsuno High School ({{Coord|32|15|06.90|N|131|33|43.93|E|region:JP-45_type:landmark|name=Ichi-no-Torii ruins}}), and its size shows the power of the past. File:Tsuno-jinja haiden.JPG|Shrine of worship File:Tsuno-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kaguraden
Old worship hall.File:Tsuno-jinja shinmon.JPG|Shinmon File:Tsuno-jinja kitaootorii.JPG|North Exit Otorii === Setsusha === === Okumiya === [[File:Tsuno-jinja Taki-jinja.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Taki Shrine
Fudo Falls in the background. ]] * '''Taki Shrine'' ** Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Nakamikami|Q644201|Takani no Mikoto}} *: It is located approximately 1km northeast of our company ({{Coord|32|16|21.07|N|131|33|04.20|E|region:JP-45_type:landmark|name=Okumiya: Taki Shrine}}). It enshrines the ``Fudo-no-taki'' waterfall that flows behind the shrine. === Sessha === *Sujin Shrine ** Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoomikoto}} *: Enshrined on the left side of the main shrine. * Asumamiku Shrine, Temasyuu Shrine ** Deity enshrined: {{jalink|Ashinazuchi Tenazuchi|Q643763|Ashima Nyuu no Mikoto}} (Ashima Nyuu Shrine), {{jalink|Ashinazuchi Tenazuchi|Q643763|Temami Nyu no Mikoto}} (Temami Nyu no Mikoto) *: To the right of the main shrine, two shrines are enshrined in one place. File:Tsuno-jinja Susanoo-jinja.JPG|Sujin Shrine File:Tsuno-jinja Ashinazuchi & Tenazuchi-jinja.JPG|Ashimyu Shrine/Temazuchi Shrine === Suesha === * Inari Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Uka no Mitama no Mikoto}} * Atago Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Fireborn Spirit}} * Kumano Shrine - Deities enshrined: {{jalink|Kumano Hayatama no Mikoto||Hatama no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kikurihime no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kikurihime no Mikoto|Q11619891|Kikurihime no Mikoto}}. The main building is the former main building of the main office. File:Tsuno-jinja Atago-jinja.JPG|Atago Shrine File:Tsuno-jinja Kumano-jinja.JPG|Kumano Shrine
The shrine building is the former main building of the headquarters. === Festival === === Annual festival ===
List of annual festivals
* Monthly ** Monthly festival (1 day) - Small festival Festivals are according to [http://www1.bbiq.jp/tunojinjya/ Annual festival list] (official website). * January ** Saitan Festival (January 1st) - Middle Festival ** New Year's Festival (January 2nd) - Small Festival ** Genshi Festival (January 3rd) - Middle Festival *February ** Setsubun Oishi Ceremony (Sunday before February 3rd) - Small festival ** Setsubun Festival (February 3rd) - Small festival ** Kigen Festival (February 11th) - Middle Festival ** '''Prayer Festival'' (February 17th) - Grand Festival ** Kumano Shrine Annual Festival (February 21st) - Small Festival * March ** Peach Blossom Festival (March 3rd) - Small Festival ** Atago Shrine Annual Festival (March 5th) - Small Festival ** Inari Shrine Spring Festival (March 17th) - Small Festival ** Taki Shrine Spring Festival (March 20th) - Small Festival ** Tsuno Soreisha Spring Sorei Festival (March 20th) - Middle Festival *April ** Kangakusai (April 10th) - Small festival ** Showa Festival (April 29th) - Small Festival ** Flower offering festival (April 29th) - Small festival * May** Kamioi Festival (May 5th) - Small Festival ** Iris Festival (Boy's Festival) (May 5th) - Small festival ** Kokuheisha Ranking Anniversary Festival (May 14th) - Small Festival * June ** Summer Purification Ceremony (June 30th) * July ** Transference Memorial Festival/Tanabata Festival (July 7th) - Small Festival ** Summer Festival Eve (July 31st) - Small Festival *August ** '''Summer Festival Mikoshi Festival'' (August 1st) - Grand Festival ** '''Summer Festival Return Festival'' (August 2nd) - Grand Festival *September ** Chongyang Festival (Longevity Festival) (September 9th) - Small Festival ** Taki Shrine Autumn Festival (September 23rd) - Small Festival ** Tsuno Soreisha Autumn Sorei Festival (September 23rd) - Middle Festival * October ** Inari Shrine Autumn Festival (October 7th) - Small Festival ** Kannaho Festival New Grain Thanksgiving (October 17th) - Middle Festival *November ** Meiji Festival (November 3rd) - Middle Festival ** Kamioi Festival (November 7th) - Small Festival ** '''Ninamesai''' (November 23rd) - Grand Festival *December ** Winter Festival Eve (December 4th) - Small Festival ** '''Reitaisai'' (December 5th) - Taisai ** Tencho Festival (December 23rd) - Middle Festival ** Soot Festival (December 27th) - Small Festival ** Grand Purification Ceremony/New Year's Eve Festival (December 31st) - Small Festival
=== Main festivals === * Summer Festival ({{jalink|August 1st|Q2788}}-{{jalink|August 2nd|Q2785|2nd}}) *: Also called the ``Goshinko Festival,'' {{jalink|Mikoshi|Q979940}} is paraded around Tsuno Town. It is said that the name comes from the fact that Empress Jingu brought the enshrined deity of the company to her boat as a guardian deity during the Sanhan Conquest, and there was a time when it was discontinued, but it was revived in {{jalink|Tenpo|Q1369045}}3 ({{jalink|1832|Q7584}}). * Winter Festival ({{jalink|December 4th|Q2310}}-{{jalink|December 5th|Q2300|5th}}) *: Kagura is played throughout the night, and the annual festival is held on the 5th. Also, on that day, it is said that if a woman suffering from breast ailments offers a ladle, she will be cured. === Cultural assets === === Tsuno-cho designated natural monument === [[File:Tsuno-jinja kusu.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Kusu (Town-designated Natural Monument)
Standing in front of the amulet awarding place. ]] * {{jalink|camphor tree|Q158722|camphor tree}} at Tsuno Shrine === Others === * Mask (wooden) - {{jalink|Qin Hesheng|Q5680877}} dedication. * Armor (Konsui) - {{jalink|Taneki Akizuki|Q11595123}} Dedicated. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Miyazaki Prefecture|Q130300}}{{jalink|Koyu District|Q1132169}}{{jalink|Tsuno Town|Q1205974}}Oaza Kawakita 13294 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{jalink|Kyushu Railway|Q498366|JR Kyushu}}{{jalink|Nippo Main Line|Q1052288}} {{jalink|Tsuno Station|Q5357711}} **Walk: about 20 minutes ** Car: Approximately 5 minutes '''Surroundings''' * Tsuno Tumulus Group === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history book * “Japanese Historical Place Name System: Place Names of Miyazaki Prefecture” ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}) Tsuno Shrine section, Tsuno Town, Koyu District * Meizo Nakamura "Tsuno Shrine" ({{jalink|Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596}} ed. "Japanese Gods -Shrines and Sacred Sites- 1 Kyushu" {{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}) * Supervised by {{jalink|Naoichi Miyaji|Q11453535}} and {{jalink|Yuyoshi Saeki|Q11382847}} "Great Dictionary of Shinto, reduced edition" {{jalink|Rinkawa Shoten|Q11612454}}, 1969 * Edited by Shikinaisha Research Association, Shikinaisha Research Report, Volume 24, Saikaido {{jalink|Kogakukan University}} Publishing Department, 1978 === Related books === * Motohiko Anzu and Yoshihiko Umeda editors and supervisors, "Shinto Dictionary", Jinja Shinposha, 1968, p. 39 * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masanori Toki, "Dictionary of Shrines" {{jalink|Tokyodo Publishing|Q11524645}}, 1979, pp. 233-234 === External link === {{Commonscat|Tsuno-jinja}} * [https://tunojinjya6.webnode.jp Hyuga Province Ichinomiya Tsuno Shrine] (official website) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/640102.html Tsuno Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsunoshinshiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsuno Shrine (Q3541675)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsuno Shrine (Q3541675)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hyūga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮崎県の神社]] [[Category:都農町の建築物]] [[Category:都農町の歴史]] [[Category:日向国の式内社]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:国幣小社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:日向国|社1]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsuno Shrine (Q3541675)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTsuno Shrine (Q3541675)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tsuno Shrine (Q3541675)}}[[Category:qq]]

{{jalink|Beppo Shrine|Q10898274}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}}6 years before enthronement |Main shrine = Ikkensha {{jalink|Raguzou|Q210684}}Copper plate roof |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{jalink|December 5th|Q2300}} |Shinto ritual = Summer festival ({{jalink|August 1st|Q2788}}-{{jalink|August 2nd|Q2785|2nd}})
Winter festival ({{jalink|December 4th|Q2310}}-{{jalink|December 5th|Q2300|5th}})|Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyazaki|label=Tsuno Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Tsuno-jinja minamitorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] ``Tsuno Shrine'' is located in {{jalink|Miyazaki Prefecture|Q130300}}{{jalink|Koyu District|Q1132169}}{{jalink|Tsuno Town|Q1205974}}. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, {{jalink|Hyuga Country|Q513737}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}. {{jalink|Modern Shrine Ranking System|Q712534|Old Shrine Ranking}} is {{jalink|Modern Shrine Ranking System|Q712534|Old Shrine}} is {{jalink|Modern Shrine Ranking System#Government Shrine|Q712534|Kokuhei-kosha}}, and currently it is {{jalink|Beppedo Shrine|Q10898274}} of {{jalink|Shinto Honcho|Q908077}}. Before {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, it was also called ``Miyazaki-sha'' and ``Miyazaki-miya''. It is also called ``Ichinomiya Shrine'' locally. === Enshrined God === * '''{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōmi Takamikoto}}''' (Onamuchi Mikoto) *: Same as Okuninushi no Mikoto. Since it has been the guardian deity of the Tsuno-cho area since ancient times, it is said that it was dedicated to the deity of the land. Furthermore, ``{{jalink|Kinbukuro|Q17213633}}'' has the text ``Tsu no Daimyojin.''There is a theory that this article in ``Kinbukuro'' is an anecdote from ``{{jalink|Fudōki|Q1194534|Hyuga Province Fudoki}},'' but this is being questioned (Iwanami Japanese Classical Literature Compendium ``Fudōki''). , ``{{jalink|Jinzoe壒壒鈔|Q30928164}}'' adds an additional note to it and states that it is a miraculous cure for {{jalink|smallpox|Q12214|smallpox}}. It was also believed that it would remove insect damage from the fields. === History === === Founding === According to the shrine's legend, {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}} began worshiping the shrine six years before his ascension to the throne, when the Emperor was en route to the {{jalink|Jinmu Tosei|Q11590444|Eastern Conquest}}. . In addition, an article in ``Jinzoe Ibukuro'' that says that {{jalink|Empress Jingu|Q232803}} solicited ``Tuno Daimyojin'' as a guardian deity for boats during the {{jalink|Sanhan Conquest|Q11249435}} This article is already in ``Jinbukuro'' and, as mentioned in the previous note, is regarded as an anecdote from ``Fudoki''. Citing , it is reported that the shrine was first built by the Empress after her triumphant return. Similarly, in ``{{jalink|Jinbukuro|Q17213633}}'', when Empress Jingu returned from the Sanhan Conquest, when she shot a bow at ``Bama-no-mine'', a black head appeared out of the soil, so she dug it up in the mountain, and two people, a man and a woman, appeared, and she summoned them as {{jalink|jinjin|Q11588676}}. In addition to ruins dating back to the {{jalink|Jomon period|Q52824}}, there are more than 20 ancient tombs (Tsuno kofun group) such as {{jalink|circular tumulus|Q11394747}} and {{jalink|keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718}}, and pottery and stone tools have been excavated near our company's shrine. It is known that people lived there from an early time, and the history of our company as a guardian deity dates back to ancient times. === Outline === {{jalink|Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jowa}} In the 4th year ({{jalink|837|Q25093}}), he was promoted to a government shrine, and in the 10th year of Jowa ({{jalink|843|Q23469}}), he was given the rank of Junior Fifth Rank (hereinafter referred to as ``{{jalink|Shoku Nippon Ki|Q1041301}}''), {{jalink|Tenan (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} In 2nd year ({{jalink|858|Q30371}}), he was promoted to Jushii (Junior Fourth Rank) ("{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}"). In "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}, ``'''Tsuno Shrine'''' is written in {{jalink|Hyuga Province|Q513737}}{{jalink|Koyu District|Q1132169}}, and it is listed as {{jalink|Shinouchi Shrine}}. It is said that at that time it was the largest shrine in Hyuga Province, with a magnificent shrine building and vast grounds. It is said that this position caused a political and religious conflict with {{jalink|Tsuman Shrine|Q3541658}}, which is listed in Tsunō Shrine in ``Japanese Gods''. As will be explained later, the details are not clear because the ancient documents were destroyed.It is certain that our company was revered not only by the common people but also by the local feudal lords as ``Ichinomiya of Hyuga Province'', and in {{jalink|Tenbun (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}}18 ({{jalink|1549|Q6554}}), the shrine building was rebuilt by {{jalink|Yoshisuke Ito|Q6095349}}. Later, in {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}}6 ({{jalink|1578|Q6707}}), during the conflict between the Otomo and Shimazu armies, {{jalink|Otomoyoshi It gradually declined after the shrine building, shrine treasures, and ancient documents were all destroyed in the fires of Chin|Q1185032|Otomo Sorin}} and {{jalink|Yoshihisa Shimazu|Q955727}}. In {{jalink|Enpo|Q1145735}} 3rd year ({{jalink|1675|Q7624}}), ``After that, there was no one to collect it, and it became a small shrine, unknown to the name, and only worshiped as a deity.'' The situation was as follows.After that, it was rebuilt in {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}} 5th year ({{jalink|1692|Q7685}}) by {{jalink|Takanabe clan|Q3034541|Takanabe clan lord}} {{jalink|Akizuki Tanemasa|Q11595121}} Since 20 koku of shrine territory was donated in the 14th year of Genroku ({{jalink|1701|Q6814}}), it has been revered by successive {{jalink|Takanabe clan|Q3034541|Takanabe feudal lord}} throughout the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}. Torii dedicated by {{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}} in 12 years ({{jalink|1727|Q6942}}) by {{jalink|Tanehiro Akizuki|Q11595118}}. The construction of the shrine in {{jalink|1736|Q6989}} by {{jalink|Tanemi Akizuki|Q11595125}} and {{ jalink|Enkyo|Q1183728}} 2nd year ({{jalink|1745|Q7586}}) Torii gate construction, {{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}}6th year ({{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}}) k|1859|Q7687}})'s {{jalink|Akizuki Tanekin|Q11595126|Dosokuin}}, the shrine building was constructed, and the lords of the domain often visited the shrine, made deputations, and offered offerings.{{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}4 ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}) In {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534}}{{jalink|Modern company rank system#government company|Q7 12534|Kokuhei-kosha}}, and in {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}9 ({{jalink|1934|Q18714}}), the area of the shrine was expanded and improved as part of the ``2600th Anniversary of Emperor Jimmu's Transfer to the East.'' After the war, it belonged to {{jalink|Shrine Honcho|Q908077}}, and in 1948 ({{jalink|1948|Q5165}}) It became Omote Shrine|Q10898274}}, and the main shrine was rebuilt in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}19 ({{jalink|2007|Q2024}}). === God level === * In August of {{jalink|Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jowa}} 4th year ({{jalink|837|Q25093}}), it was promoted to a government shrine (``{{jalink|Shoku Nihon Koki|Q1041301}}'') - It is written as ``Shitonojin.'' *September 19, 10th year of Jowa ({{jalink|843|Q23469}}), from no rank to junior fifth rank ("Shoku Nihon Koki") - Written as "Tsuno Kojin". * {{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} October 22, 2nd year ({{jalink|858|Q30371}}), from the upper fifth rank to the upper fourth rank (``{{jalink|Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981}}'') === Precincts === [[File:Tsuno-jinja honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|main hall]] The main shrine is {{jalink|Nagaruzou|Q210684|Ikkensha Nagaruzou}} with a copper plate roof and {{jalink|Chigi/Katsuoki|Q87220234}}. It was rebuilt in 2007 and largely follows the shape of the old main shrine. The old main shrine was rebuilt in 1986 with a donation from Kinosuke Kono, and it has now been relocated and serves as the main shrine of Kumano Shrine, a subsidiary shrine. The worship hall, like the main shrine, was rebuilt in 2007. It is a {{jalink|irimoya-zukuri |Q855025}} with 5 rooms in the front and 3 spaces on the sides, and a {{jalink|gable-zukuri |Q13308575}} tsumairi shrine with a depth of 2 ken in the center of the back. The roof is made of copper sheets, with a {{jalink|gable #form of gables|Q4881511|staggered gable}} on the front, and below that is a three-room {{jalink|facing gable|Q11415970}} with a gable and a gable in the center. The old worship hall has been relocated and is now used as a kagura hall. In ancient times, the Ichi-no-Torii gate was located near Tsuno High School ({{Coord|32|15|06.90|N|131|33|43.93|E|region:JP-45_type:landmark|name=Ichi-no-Torii ruins}}), and its size shows the power of the past. Old worship hall.File:Tsuno-jinja shinmon.JPG|Shinmon File:Tsuno-jinja kitaootorii.JPG|North Exit Otorii === Setsusha === === Okumiya === [[File:Tsuno-jinja Taki-jinja.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Taki Shrine
Fudo Falls in the background. ]] * '''Taki Shrine'' ** Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Nakamikami|Q644201|Takani no Mikoto}} *: It is located approximately 1km northeast of our company ({{Coord|32|16|21.07|N|131|33|04.20|E|region:JP-45_type:landmark|name=Okumiya: Taki Shrine}}). It enshrines the ``Fudo-no-taki'' waterfall that flows behind the shrine. === Sessha === *Sujin Shrine ** Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoomikoto}} *: Enshrined on the left side of the main shrine. * Asumamiku Shrine, Temasyuu Shrine ** Deity enshrined: {{jalink|Ashinazuchi Tenazuchi|Q643763|Ashima Nyuu no Mikoto}} (Ashima Nyuu Shrine), {{jalink|Ashinazuchi Tenazuchi|Q643763|Temami Nyu no Mikoto}} (Temami Nyu no Mikoto) *: To the right of the main shrine, two shrines are enshrined in one place. File:Tsuno-jinja Susanoo-jinja.JPG|Sujin Shrine File:Tsuno-jinja Ashinazuchi & Tenazuchi-jinja.JPG|Ashimyu Shrine/Temazuchi Shrine === Suesha === * Inari Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Uka no Mitama no Mikoto}} * Atago Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Fireborn Spirit}} * Kumano Shrine - Deities enshrined: {{jalink|Kumano Hayatama no Mikoto||Hatama no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kikurihime no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kikurihime no Mikoto|Q11619891|Kikurihime no Mikoto}}. The main building is the former main building of the main office. File:Tsuno-jinja Atago-jinja.JPG|Atago Shrine File:Tsuno-jinja Kumano-jinja.JPG|Kumano Shrine

=== Main festivals === * Summer Festival ({{jalink|August 1st|Q2788}}-{{jalink|August 2nd|Q2785|2nd}}) *: Also called the ``Goshinko Festival,'' {{jalink|Mikoshi|Q979940}} is paraded around Tsuno Town. It is said that the name comes from the fact that Empress Jingu brought the enshrined deity of the company to her boat as a guardian deity during the Sanhan Conquest, and there was a time when it was discontinued, but it was revived in {{jalink|Tenpo|Q1369045}}3 ({{jalink|1832|Q7584}}). * Winter Festival ({{jalink|December 4th|Q2310}}-{{jalink|December 5th|Q2300|5th}}) *: Kagura is played throughout the night, and the annual festival is held on the 5th. Also, on that day, it is said that if a woman suffering from breast ailments offers a ladle, she will be cured. === Cultural assets === === Tsuno-cho designated natural monument === [[File:Tsuno-jinja kusu.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Kusu (Town-designated Natural Monument)
Standing in front of the amulet awarding place. ]] * {{jalink|camphor tree|Q158722|camphor tree}} at Tsuno Shrine === Others === * Mask (wooden) - {{jalink|Qin Hesheng|Q5680877}} dedication. * Armor (Konsui) - {{jalink|Taneki Akizuki|Q11595123}} Dedicated. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Miyazaki Prefecture|Q130300}}{{jalink|Koyu District|Q1132169}}{{jalink|Tsuno Town|Q1205974}}Oaza Kawakita 13294 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{jalink|Kyushu Railway|Q498366|JR Kyushu}}{{jalink|Nippo Main Line|Q1052288}} {{jalink|Tsuno Station|Q5357711}} **Walk: about 20 minutes ** Car: Approximately 5 minutes '''Surroundings''' * Tsuno Tumulus Group === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history book * “Japanese Historical Place Name System: Place Names of Miyazaki Prefecture” ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}) Tsuno Shrine section, Tsuno Town, Koyu District * Meizo Nakamura "Tsuno Shrine" ({{jalink|Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596}} ed. Tsuno Shrine |Image = [[File:Tsuno-jinja keidai.JPG|270px]]
Precincts (prayer hall on the left, Kumano Shrine, a subsidiary shrine on the right) |Location = {{jalink|Miyazaki Prefecture|Q130300}}{{jalink|Koyu District|Q1132169}}{{jalink|Tsuno Town|Q1205974}}Oaza Kawakita 13294 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 degrees minutes, seconds|32|15|47.2|N|131|33|31.2|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Okuninushi|Q276944|Okumi no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Shaku = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)

{{jalink|Beppo Shrine|Q10898274}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}}6 years before enthronement |Main shrine = Ikkensha {{jalink|Raguzou|Q210684}}Copper plate roof |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{jalink|December 5th|Q2300}} |Shinto ritual = Summer festival ({{jalink|August 1st|Q2788}}-{{jalink|August 2nd|Q2785|2nd}})
Winter festival ({{jalink|December 4th|Q2310}}-{{jalink|December 5th|Q2300|5th}})|Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyazaki|label=Tsuno Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Tsuno-jinja minamitorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] ``Tsuno Shrine'' is located in {{jalink|Miyazaki Prefecture|Q130300}}{{jalink|Koyu District|Q1132169}}{{jalink|Tsuno Town|Q1205974}}. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, {{jalink|Hyuga Country|Q513737}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}. {{jalink|Modern Shrine Ranking System|Q712534|Old Shrine Ranking}} is {{jalink|Modern Shrine Ranking System|Q712534|Old Shrine}} is {{jalink|Modern Shrine Ranking System#Government Shrine|Q712534|Kokuhei-kosha}}, and currently it is {{jalink|Beppedo Shrine|Q10898274}} of {{jalink|Shinto Honcho|Q908077}}. Before {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, it was also called ``Miyazaki-sha'' and ``Miyazaki-miya''. It is also called ``Ichinomiya Shrine'' locally. === Enshrined God === * '''{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōmi Takamikoto}}''' (Onamuchi Mikoto) *: Same as Okuninushi no Mikoto. Since it has been the guardian deity of the Tsuno-cho area since ancient times, it is said that it was dedicated to the deity of the land. Furthermore, ``{{jalink|Kinbukuro|Q17213633}}'' has the text ``Tsu no Daimyojin.''There is a theory that this article in ``Kinbukuro'' is an anecdote from ``{{jalink|Fudōki|Q1194534|Hyuga Province Fudoki}},'' but this is being questioned (Iwanami Japanese Classical Literature Compendium ``Fudōki''). , ``{{jalink|Jinzoe壒壒鈔|Q30928164}}'' adds an additional note to it and states that it is a miraculous cure for {{jalink|smallpox|Q12214|smallpox}}. It was also believed that it would remove insect damage from the fields. === History === === Founding === According to the shrine's legend, {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}} began worshiping the shrine six years before his ascension to the throne, when the Emperor was en route to the {{jalink|Jinmu Tosei|Q11590444|Eastern Conquest}}. . In addition, an article in ``Jinzoe Ibukuro'' that says that {{jalink|Empress Jingu|Q232803}} solicited ``Tuno Daimyojin'' as a guardian deity for boats during the {{jalink|Sanhan Conquest|Q11249435}} This article is already in ``Jinbukuro'' and, as mentioned in the previous note, is regarded as an anecdote from ``Fudoki''. Citing , it is reported that the shrine was first built by the Empress after her triumphant return. Similarly, in ``{{jalink|Jinbukuro|Q17213633}}'', when Empress Jingu returned from the Sanhan Conquest, when she shot a bow at ``Bama-no-mine'', a black head appeared out of the soil, so she dug it up in the mountain, and two people, a man and a woman, appeared, and she summoned them as {{jalink|jinjin|Q11588676}}. In addition to ruins dating back to the {{jalink|Jomon period|Q52824}}, there are more than 20 ancient tombs (Tsuno kofun group) such as {{jalink|circular tumulus|Q11394747}} and {{jalink|keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718}}, and pottery and stone tools have been excavated near our company's shrine. It is known that people lived there from an early time, and the history of our company as a guardian deity dates back to ancient times. === Outline === {{jalink|Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jowa}} In the 4th year ({{jalink|837|Q25093}}), he was promoted to a government shrine, and in the 10th year of Jowa ({{jalink|843|Q23469}}), he was given the rank of Junior Fifth Rank (hereinafter referred to as ``{{jalink|Shoku Nippon Ki|Q1041301}}''), {{jalink|Tenan (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} In 2nd year ({{jalink|858|Q30371}}), he was promoted to Jushii (Junior Fourth Rank) ("{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}"). In "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}, ``'''Tsuno Shrine'''' is written in {{jalink|Hyuga Province|Q513737}}{{jalink|Koyu District|Q1132169}}, and it is listed as {{jalink|Shinouchi Shrine}}. It is said that at that time it was the largest shrine in Hyuga Province, with a magnificent shrine building and vast grounds. It is said that this position caused a political and religious conflict with {{jalink|Tsuman Shrine|Q3541658}}, which is listed in Tsunō Shrine in ``Japanese Gods''. As will be explained later, the details are not clear because the ancient documents were destroyed.It is certain that our company was revered not only by the common people but also by the local feudal lords as ``Ichinomiya of Hyuga Province'', and in {{jalink|Tenbun (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}}18 ({{jalink|1549|Q6554}}), the shrine building was rebuilt by {{jalink|Yoshisuke Ito|Q6095349}}. Later, in {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}}6 ({{jalink|1578|Q6707}}), during the conflict between the Otomo and Shimazu armies, {{jalink|Otomoyoshi It gradually declined after the shrine building, shrine treasures, and ancient documents were all destroyed in the fires of Chin|Q1185032|Otomo Sorin}} and {{jalink|Yoshihisa Shimazu|Q955727}}. In {{jalink|Enpo|Q1145735}} 3rd year ({{jalink|1675|Q7624}}), ``After that, there was no one to collect it, and it became a small shrine, unknown to the name, and only worshiped as a deity.'' The situation was as follows.After that, it was rebuilt in {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}} 5th year ({{jalink|1692|Q7685}}) by {{jalink|Takanabe clan|Q3034541|Takanabe clan lord}} {{jalink|Akizuki Tanemasa|Q11595121}} Since 20 koku of shrine territory was donated in the 14th year of Genroku ({{jalink|1701|Q6814}}), it has been revered by successive {{jalink|Takanabe clan|Q3034541|Takanabe feudal lord}} throughout the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}. Torii dedicated by {{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}} in 12 years ({{jalink|1727|Q6942}}) by {{jalink|Tanehiro Akizuki|Q11595118}}. The construction of the shrine in {{jalink|1736|Q6989}} by {{jalink|Tanemi Akizuki|Q11595125}} and {{ jalink|Enkyo|Q1183728}} 2nd year ({{jalink|1745|Q7586}}) Torii gate construction, {{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}}6th year ({{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}}) k|1859|Q7687}})'s {{jalink|Akizuki Tanekin|Q11595126|Dosokuin}}, the shrine building was constructed, and the lords of the domain often visited the shrine, made deputations, and offered offerings.{{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}4 ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}) In {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534}}{{jalink|Modern company rank system#government company|Q7 12534|Kokuhei-kosha}}, and in {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}9 ({{jalink|1934|Q18714}}), the area of the shrine was expanded and improved as part of the ``2600th Anniversary of Emperor Jimmu's Transfer to the East.'' After the war, it belonged to {{jalink|Shrine Honcho|Q908077}}, and in 1948 ({{jalink|1948|Q5165}}) It became Omote Shrine|Q10898274}}, and the main shrine was rebuilt in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}19 ({{jalink|2007|Q2024}}). === God level === * In August of {{jalink|Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jowa}} 4th year ({{jalink|837|Q25093}}), it was promoted to a government shrine (``{{jalink|Shoku Nihon Koki|Q1041301}}'') - It is written as ``Shitonojin.'' *September 19, 10th year of Jowa ({{jalink|843|Q23469}}), from no rank to junior fifth rank ("Shoku Nihon Koki") - Written as "Tsuno Kojin". * {{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} October 22, 2nd year ({{jalink|858|Q30371}}), from the upper fifth rank to the upper fourth rank (``{{jalink|Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981}}'') === Precincts === [[File:Tsuno-jinja honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|main hall]] The main shrine is {{jalink|Nagaruzou|Q210684|Ikkensha Nagaruzou}} with a copper plate roof and {{jalink|Chigi/Katsuoki|Q87220234}}. It was rebuilt in 2007 and largely follows the shape of the old main shrine. The old main shrine was rebuilt in 1986 with a donation from Kinosuke Kono, and it has now been relocated and serves as the main shrine of Kumano Shrine, a subsidiary shrine. The worship hall, like the main shrine, was rebuilt in 2007. It is a {{jalink|irimoya-zukuri |Q855025}} with 5 rooms in the front and 3 spaces on the sides, and a {{jalink|gable-zukuri |Q13308575}} tsumairi shrine with a depth of 2 ken in the center of the back. The roof is made of copper sheets, with a {{jalink|gable #form of gables|Q4881511|staggered gable}} on the front, and below that is a three-room {{jalink|facing gable|Q11415970}} with a gable and a gable in the center. The old worship hall has been relocated and is now used as a kagura hall. In ancient times, the Ichi-no-Torii gate was located near Tsuno High School ({{Coord|32|15|06.90|N|131|33|43.93|E|region:JP-45_type:landmark|name=Ichi-no-Torii ruins}}), and its size shows the power of the past. File:Tsuno-jinja haiden.JPG|Shrine of worship File:Tsuno-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kaguraden
Old worship hall.File:Tsuno-jinja shinmon.JPG|Shinmon File:Tsuno-jinja kitaootorii.JPG|North Exit Otorii === Setsusha === === Okumiya === [[File:Tsuno-jinja Taki-jinja.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Taki Shrine
Fudo Falls in the background. ]] * '''Taki Shrine'' ** Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Nakamikami|Q644201|Takani no Mikoto}} *: It is located approximately 1km northeast of our company ({{Coord|32|16|21.07|N|131|33|04.20|E|region:JP-45_type:landmark|name=Okumiya: Taki Shrine}}). It enshrines the ``Fudo-no-taki'' waterfall that flows behind the shrine. === Sessha === *Sujin Shrine ** Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoomikoto}} *: Enshrined on the left side of the main shrine. * Asumamiku Shrine, Temasyuu Shrine ** Deity enshrined: {{jalink|Ashinazuchi Tenazuchi|Q643763|Ashima Nyuu no Mikoto}} (Ashima Nyuu Shrine), {{jalink|Ashinazuchi Tenazuchi|Q643763|Temami Nyu no Mikoto}} (Temami Nyu no Mikoto) *: To the right of the main shrine, two shrines are enshrined in one place. File:Tsuno-jinja Susanoo-jinja.JPG|Sujin Shrine File:Tsuno-jinja Ashinazuchi & Tenazuchi-jinja.JPG|Ashimyu Shrine/Temazuchi Shrine === Suesha === * Inari Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Uka no Mitama no Mikoto}} * Atago Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Fireborn Spirit}} * Kumano Shrine - Deities enshrined: {{jalink|Kumano Hayatama no Mikoto||Hatama no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kikurihime no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kikurihime no Mikoto|Q11619891|Kikurihime no Mikoto}}. The main building is the former main building of the main office. File:Tsuno-jinja Atago-jinja.JPG|Atago Shrine File:Tsuno-jinja Kumano-jinja.JPG|Kumano Shrine
The shrine building is the former main building of the headquarters. === Festival === === Annual festival ===

=== Main festivals === * Summer Festival ({{jalink|August 1st|Q2788}}-{{jalink|August 2nd|Q2785|2nd}}) *: Also called the ``Goshinko Festival,'' {{jalink|Mikoshi|Q979940}} is paraded around Tsuno Town. It is said that the name comes from the fact that Empress Jingu brought the enshrined deity of the company to her boat as a guardian deity during the Sanhan Conquest, and there was a time when it was discontinued, but it was revived in {{jalink|Tenpo|Q1369045}}3 ({{jalink|1832|Q7584}}). * Winter Festival ({{jalink|December 4th|Q2310}}-{{jalink|December 5th|Q2300|5th}}) *: Kagura is played throughout the night, and the annual festival is held on the 5th. Also, on that day, it is said that if a woman suffering from breast ailments offers a ladle, she will be cured. === Cultural assets === === Tsuno-cho designated natural monument === [[File:Tsuno-jinja kusu.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Kusu (Town-designated Natural Monument)
Standing in front of the amulet awarding place. ]] * {{jalink|camphor tree|Q158722|camphor tree}} at Tsuno Shrine === Others === * Mask (wooden) - {{jalink|Qin Hesheng|Q5680877}} dedication. * Armor (Konsui) - {{jalink|Taneki Akizuki|Q11595123}} Dedicated. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Miyazaki Prefecture|Q130300}}{{jalink|Koyu District|Q1132169}}{{jalink|Tsuno Town|Q1205974}}Oaza Kawakita 13294 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{jalink|Kyushu Railway|Q498366|JR Kyushu}}{{jalink|Nippo Main Line|Q1052288}} {{jalink|Tsuno Station|Q5357711}} **Walk: about 20 minutes ** Car: Approximately 5 minutes '''Surroundings''' * Tsuno Tumulus Group === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history book * “Japanese Historical Place Name System: Place Names of Miyazaki Prefecture” ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}) Tsuno Shrine section, Tsuno Town, Koyu District * Meizo Nakamura "Tsuno Shrine" ({{jalink|Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596}} ed. Winter festival ({{jalink|December 4th|Q2310}}-{{jalink|December 5th|Q2300|5th}})|Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyazaki|label=Tsuno Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Tsuno-jinja minamitorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] ``Tsuno Shrine'' is located in {{jalink|Miyazaki Prefecture|Q130300}}{{jalink|Koyu District|Q1132169}}{{jalink|Tsuno Town|Q1205974}}. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, {{jalink|Hyuga Country|Q513737}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}. {{jalink|Modern Shrine Ranking System|Q712534|Old Shrine Ranking}} is {{jalink|Modern Shrine Ranking System|Q712534|Old Shrine}} is {{jalink|Modern Shrine Ranking System#Government Shrine|Q712534|Kokuhei-kosha}}, and currently it is {{jalink|Beppedo Shrine|Q10898274}} of {{jalink|Shinto Honcho|Q908077}}. Before {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, it was also called ``Miyazaki-sha'' and ``Miyazaki-miya''. It is also called ``Ichinomiya Shrine'' locally. === Enshrined God === * '''{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōmi Takamikoto}}''' (Onamuchi Mikoto) *: Same as Okuninushi no Mikoto. Since it has been the guardian deity of the Tsuno-cho area since ancient times, it is said that it was dedicated to the deity of the land. Furthermore, ``{{jalink|Kinbukuro|Q17213633}}'' has the text ``Tsu no Daimyojin.''There is a theory that this article in ``Kinbukuro'' is an anecdote from ``{{jalink|Fudōki|Q1194534|Hyuga Province Fudoki}},'' but this is being questioned (Iwanami Japanese Classical Literature Compendium ``Fudōki''). , ``{{jalink|Jinzoe壒壒鈔|Q30928164}}'' adds an additional note to it and states that it is a miraculous cure for {{jalink|smallpox|Q12214|smallpox}}. It was also believed that it would remove insect damage from the fields. === History === === Founding === According to the shrine's legend, {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}} began worshiping the shrine six years before his ascension to the throne, when the Emperor was en route to the {{jalink|Jinmu Tosei|Q11590444|Eastern Conquest}}. . In addition, an article in ``Jinzoe Ibukuro'' that says that {{jalink|Empress Jingu|Q232803}} solicited ``Tuno Daimyojin'' as a guardian deity for boats during the {{jalink|Sanhan Conquest|Q11249435}} This article is already in ``Jinbukuro'' and, as mentioned in the previous note, is regarded as an anecdote from ``Fudoki''. Citing , it is reported that the shrine was first built by the Empress after her triumphant return. Similarly, in ``{{jalink|Jinbukuro|Q17213633}}'', when Empress Jingu returned from the Sanhan Conquest, when she shot a bow at ``Bama-no-mine'', a black head appeared out of the soil, so she dug it up in the mountain, and two people, a man and a woman, appeared, and she summoned them as {{jalink|jinjin|Q11588676}}. In addition to ruins dating back to the {{jalink|Jomon period|Q52824}}, there are more than 20 ancient tombs (Tsuno kofun group) such as {{jalink|circular tumulus|Q11394747}} and {{jalink|keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718}}, and pottery and stone tools have been excavated near our company's shrine. It is known that people lived there from an early time, and the history of our company as a guardian deity dates back to ancient times. === Outline === {{jalink|Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jowa}} In the 4th year ({{jalink|837|Q25093}}), he was promoted to a government shrine, and in the 10th year of Jowa ({{jalink|843|Q23469}}), he was given the rank of Junior Fifth Rank (hereinafter referred to as ``{{jalink|Shoku Nippon Ki|Q1041301}}''), {{jalink|Tenan (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} In 2nd year ({{jalink|858|Q30371}}), he was promoted to Jushii (Junior Fourth Rank) ("{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}"). In "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}, ``'''Tsuno Shrine'''' is written in {{jalink|Hyuga Province|Q513737}}{{jalink|Koyu District|Q1132169}}, and it is listed as {{jalink|Shinouchi Shrine}}. It is said that at that time it was the largest shrine in Hyuga Province, with a magnificent shrine building and vast grounds. It is said that this position caused a political and religious conflict with {{jalink|Tsuman Shrine|Q3541658}}, which is listed in Tsunō Shrine in ``Japanese Gods''. As will be explained later, the details are not clear because the ancient documents were destroyed.It is certain that our company was revered not only by the common people but also by the local feudal lords as ``Ichinomiya of Hyuga Province'', and in {{jalink|Tenbun (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}}18 ({{jalink|1549|Q6554}}), the shrine building was rebuilt by {{jalink|Yoshisuke Ito|Q6095349}}. Later, in {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}}6 ({{jalink|1578|Q6707}}), during the conflict between the Otomo and Shimazu armies, {{jalink|Otomoyoshi It gradually declined after the shrine building, shrine treasures, and ancient documents were all destroyed in the fires of Chin|Q1185032|Otomo Sorin}} and {{jalink|Yoshihisa Shimazu|Q955727}}. In {{jalink|Enpo|Q1145735}} 3rd year ({{jalink|1675|Q7624}}), ``After that, there was no one to collect it, and it became a small shrine, unknown to the name, and only worshiped as a deity.'' The situation was as follows.After that, it was rebuilt in {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}} 5th year ({{jalink|1692|Q7685}}) by {{jalink|Takanabe clan|Q3034541|Takanabe clan lord}} {{jalink|Akizuki Tanemasa|Q11595121}} Since 20 koku of shrine territory was donated in the 14th year of Genroku ({{jalink|1701|Q6814}}), it has been revered by successive {{jalink|Takanabe clan|Q3034541|Takanabe feudal lord}} throughout the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}. Torii dedicated by {{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}} in 12 years ({{jalink|1727|Q6942}}) by {{jalink|Tanehiro Akizuki|Q11595118}}. The construction of the shrine in {{jalink|1736|Q6989}} by {{jalink|Tanemi Akizuki|Q11595125}} and {{ jalink|Enkyo|Q1183728}} 2nd year ({{jalink|1745|Q7586}}) Torii gate construction, {{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}}6th year ({{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}}) k|1859|Q7687}})'s {{jalink|Akizuki Tanekin|Q11595126|Dosokuin}}, the shrine building was constructed, and the lords of the domain often visited the shrine, made deputations, and offered offerings.{{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}4 ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}) In {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534}}{{jalink|Modern company rank system#government company|Q7 12534|Kokuhei-kosha}}, and in {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}9 ({{jalink|1934|Q18714}}), the area of the shrine was expanded and improved as part of the ``2600th Anniversary of Emperor Jimmu's Transfer to the East.'' After the war, it belonged to {{jalink|Shrine Honcho|Q908077}}, and in 1948 ({{jalink|1948|Q5165}}) It became Omote Shrine|Q10898274}}, and the main shrine was rebuilt in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}19 ({{jalink|2007|Q2024}}). === God level === * In August of {{jalink|Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jowa}} 4th year ({{jalink|837|Q25093}}), it was promoted to a government shrine (``{{jalink|Shoku Nihon Koki|Q1041301}}'') - It is written as ``Shitonojin.'' *September 19, 10th year of Jowa ({{jalink|843|Q23469}}), from no rank to junior fifth rank ("Shoku Nihon Koki") - Written as "Tsuno Kojin". * {{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} October 22, 2nd year ({{jalink|858|Q30371}}), from the upper fifth rank to the upper fourth rank (``{{jalink|Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981}}'') === Precincts === [[File:Tsuno-jinja honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|main hall]] The main shrine is {{jalink|Nagaruzou|Q210684|Ikkensha Nagaruzou}} with a copper plate roof and {{jalink|Chigi/Katsuoki|Q87220234}}. It was rebuilt in 2007 and largely follows the shape of the old main shrine. The old main shrine was rebuilt in 1986 with a donation from Kinosuke Kono, and it has now been relocated and serves as the main shrine of Kumano Shrine, a subsidiary shrine. The worship hall, like the main shrine, was rebuilt in 2007. It is a {{jalink|irimoya-zukuri |Q855025}} with 5 rooms in the front and 3 spaces on the sides, and a {{jalink|gable-zukuri |Q13308575}} tsumairi shrine with a depth of 2 ken in the center of the back. The roof is made of copper sheets, with a {{jalink|gable #form of gables|Q4881511|staggered gable}} on the front, and below that is a three-room {{jalink|facing gable|Q11415970}} with a gable and a gable in the center. The old worship hall has been relocated and is now used as a kagura hall. In ancient times, the Ichi-no-Torii gate was located near Tsuno High School ({{Coord|32|15|06.90|N|131|33|43.93|E|region:JP-45_type:landmark|name=Ichi-no-Torii ruins}}), and its size shows the power of the past. Fudo Falls in the background. ]] * '''Taki Shrine'' ** Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Nakamikami|Q644201|Takani no Mikoto}} *: It is located approximately 1km northeast of our company ({{Coord|32|16|21.07|N|131|33|04.20|E|region:JP-45_type:landmark|name=Okumiya: Taki Shrine}}). It enshrines the ``Fudo-no-taki'' waterfall that flows behind the shrine. === Sessha === *Sujin Shrine ** Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoomikoto}} *: Enshrined on the left side of the main shrine. * Asumamiku Shrine, Temasyuu Shrine ** Deity enshrined: {{jalink|Ashinazuchi Tenazuchi|Q643763|Ashima Nyuu no Mikoto}} (Ashima Nyuu Shrine), {{jalink|Ashinazuchi Tenazuchi|Q643763|Temami Nyu no Mikoto}} (Temami Nyu no Mikoto) *: To the right of the main shrine, two shrines are enshrined in one place. File:Tsuno-jinja Susanoo-jinja.JPG|Sujin Shrine File:Tsuno-jinja Ashinazuchi & Tenazuchi-jinja.JPG|Ashimyu Shrine/Temazuchi Shrine === Suesha === * Inari Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Uka no Mitama no Mikoto}} * Atago Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Fireborn Spirit}} * Kumano Shrine - Deities enshrined: {{jalink|Kumano Hayatama no Mikoto||Hatama no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kikurihime no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kikurihime no Mikoto|Q11619891|Kikurihime no Mikoto}}. The main building is the former main building of the main office. File:Tsuno-jinja Atago-jinja.JPG|Atago Shrine File:Tsuno-jinja Kumano-jinja.JPG|Kumano Shrine

=== Main festivals === * Summer Festival ({{jalink|August 1st|Q2788}}-{{jalink|August 2nd|Q2785|2nd}}) *: Also called the ``Goshinko Festival,'' {{jalink|Mikoshi|Q979940}} is paraded around Tsuno Town. It is said that the name comes from the fact that Empress Jingu brought the enshrined deity of the company to her boat as a guardian deity during the Sanhan Conquest, and there was a time when it was discontinued, but it was revived in {{jalink|Tenpo|Q1369045}}3 ({{jalink|1832|Q7584}}). * Winter Festival ({{jalink|December 4th|Q2310}}-{{jalink|December 5th|Q2300|5th}}) *: Kagura is played throughout the night, and the annual festival is held on the 5th. Also, on that day, it is said that if a woman suffering from breast ailments offers a ladle, she will be cured. === Cultural assets === === Tsuno-cho designated natural monument === [[File:Tsuno-jinja kusu.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Kusu (Town-designated Natural Monument)
